topic: French municipal elections abstention historical data
summary: Historical first-round abstention rates for French municipal elections
content:
Historical first round abstention rates (municipal elections):
- 1983: ~21.6% (participation ~78.4%)
- 1989: ~27.2% (participation ~72.8%)
- 1995: ~30.6% (participation ~69.4%)
- 2001: ~32.5-34.5% (participation varies by source)
- 2008: ~33.5-35.1% (participation varies by source)
- 2014: ~36.5% (participation ~63.5%)
- 2020: ~55.3% (participation ~44.7%) - COVID anomaly

The upward trend from 1983-2014 is clear: roughly +3-4pp per election cycle (6 years each).
Trend extrapolation to 2026 without COVID: ~39-42%

Question says pre-COVID trend reversion would be ~38-42%.

Key 2020 detail: 39% of abstentionists cited COVID as primary reason.
2020 second round (June 28): 59% abstention.
